mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) sits below 0.25%, the zero lower bound amplifies equity risk premium compression from unconventional policy expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (annualized Sharpe < 0.2) in > 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market that boosts consumption forecasts; this joint pattern predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days more strongly than either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and UNRATE adds no incremental explanatory power, evidenced by an F-test p-value > 0.05 for the interaction coefficient in a 20-day return regression, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal demand recovery before it is priced into broad indices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship vanishes when unemployment is low.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.0%, positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal underperforms the unconditional baseline by > 2pp annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) to a new 12-month low signals severe capacity underutilization and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months when INDPRO hits a new 12-month low.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and rising unemployment measured by UNRATE constitute stagflationary pressure that compresses real earnings and forces valuation multiple contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL and UNRATE interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns, or the interaction signal Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200,000 signals above-trend labor demand that reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when PAYEMS rises by > 200,000 in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward S&P 500 returns following positive QoQ GDPC1 growth are not positive in > 55% of quarters, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.
